index.title: Orthodox Apostolic Succession — timelines
index.subtitle: Succession-of-sees timelines with historical context band. See also the map of sees over time and the interactive consecration graph (the other succession model — never conflated with this one). Regenerated from build/site-data/.
calendar.note: Generated from sourced veneration.feast_days. Feasts recorded on the Julian calendar fall 13 civil days later; the toggle switches how the date column reads. Display:
calendar.none: no commemorations recorded for this date (the veneration layer is populated opportunistically — see DATA_COMPLETION §1)
map.title: Map — Orthodox Apostolic Succession
map.subtitle: ← timelines · consecration graph · Every see with recorded coordinates, shown as the time slider's year sees it. Fill = the status of the tenure active that year; hollow = attested by then but no recorded occupant that year. Click an occupied see for the bishop's profile.
map.legend.attested: attested, no recorded occupant that year
map.note: Coverage reflects the dataset, not history: a hollow circle often means the succession list for that era is still to be entered (see the gap reports). Basemap: Natural Earth via world-atlas, bundled — no external requests.
map.footer: Data: CC BY 4.0 · Locations are see coordinates from the data layer (ROADMAP A2); the map never invents jurisdictional boundaries — points only.
badge.corroborated.tip: Two or more independent non-tradition sources cite this record (ROADMAP C4).
index.timelines.note: Bars are tenures (see-succession). Hatched right edge = end of tenure unrecorded. The top band is the curated context layer — background only, never part of any succession chain. Click a bar for the bishop's profile.
index.footer: Data: CC BY 4.0 · Code: MIT · Every record carries graded citations; unverified means a human has not yet confirmed the claim against a graded source. Recognition disputes are recorded, not adjudicated.
graph.title: Consecration graph — Orthodox Apostolic Succession
graph.subtitle: ← timelines · Who laid hands on whom consecration-succession — a directed acyclic graph, kept strictly apart from the succession of sees. Drag nodes, scroll to zoom, click a node for the bishop's profile.
graph.stats: {bishops} bishops · {edges} consecration edge(s) · {writers} with surviving writings — only persons with consecration records appear.
graph.footer: Absent consecration data is stated, never inferred — bishops without a consecration record simply do not appear here. Seeded edges (Wikidata P1598) do not distinguish principal from co-consecrators and render dashed until verified.
person.consecration.none: No consecration data recorded — absent, not inferred.
person.footer: Data: CC BY 4.0 · The two succession models (occupancy of sees vs. laying-on of hands) are shown separately and never conflated. Absent consecration data is stated, not inferred.
error.load: Could not load data: {msg}. Run the build (make build / build.ps1) and serve the repo root over HTTP: python -m http.server, then open http://localhost:8000/site/.
